user_message: I need you to process this email thread from Tailspin Toys. The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-02-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on pipeline hygiene from our last session.

[00:05:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:07:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:14:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:18:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:21:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-F trap (Skips compliance/regulatory blockers when mentioned); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-F failure mode (Skips compliance/regulatory blockers when mentioned)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-F -- Skips compliance/regulatory blockers when mentioned
